data.description: Currently the blinking/animating cursor inside the search input only animates while the input is focused/active. User wants the cursor animation to play CONTINUOUSLY — even when the user hasn't clicked into the search bar — to draw attention to the search affordance. Applies to BOTH: (1) home page search bar, (2) publications page search bar. Scope: locate the search input components and the cursor animation logic (likely a pseudo-element or a wrapper span with a CSS animation gated on :focus or a focused state). Remove the focus-gating so the animation runs unconditionally. Verify the animation doesn't conflict with the real text caret when the user actually types. Acceptance: cursor animation is visible on page load on both home and publications pages without the user interacting; still behaves correctly once the user starts typing.
data.title: Search bar cursor animation: keep animating even when not focused (home + publications)

data.body: Review pass. Diff matches plan exactly: removed {isFocused && (...)} wrapper around the decorative cursor overlay span in src/components/sections/Hero.tsx (line 218 area) and src/components/lab/ArchiveSearch.tsx (line 106 area). Verified preserved: isFocused state declarations, useLayoutEffect deps ([query, isFocused] in Hero, [value, isFocused] in ArchiveSearch), and inline caretColor: transparent on both inputs — no double-cursor risk. No collateral edits, no CSS changes. pnpm typecheck clean. Visual blink-on-load verification across / and /lab deferred to human per workflow. Implementation meets acceptance criteria for code-level checks.
